title: Comment on: Short pulse width widens the therapeutic window of subthalamic neurostimulation
journal_name: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
study_question: Does the assertion that a short pulse width widens the therapeutic window of subthalamic neurostimulation hold true when data is re-analyzed based on charge per pulse?
population_assessed: A small number of patients from a previous study by Reich et al. undergoing subthalamic neurostimulation.
outcome_measures: Therapeutic window (difference in current between rigidity control and adverse muscle contractions), charge per pulse, rigidity control, muscle contraction
answer_to_study_question: When data is replotted in terms of charge per pulse, the therapeutic window decreases for narrower pulses, contrary to the original claim. Wider pulses result in a larger therapeutic window.
